acts.title: The Basement

tension_arc.narrative_note: Open with an unsettling mundane detail.

acts.script_draft: Narrator: It started as an academic exercise. Twenty-four young men answered an ad in the Stanford student newspaper. Five dollars a day. A study about prison life. What could go wrong?

acts.title: The Rules Begin to Bend
acts.script_draft: Narrator: By hour thirty-six, prisoner 8612 was screaming. Not performing. Screaming. Zimbardo watched through the observation window. He did not stop the experiment.

acts.title: The Superintendent
acts.script_draft: Narrator: The most dangerous thing Philip Zimbardo did was not design the experiment. It was forget he was running it.

acts.title: The Question That Ended It
acts.script_draft: Narrator: Forty years later, tapes surfaced. The guards had been told to be tough. The spontaneous evil was scripted.

acts.title: What Remains
acts.script_draft: Narrator: The experiment that claimed to prove anyone could become a monster proved something else entirely. That even scientists are not immune to the roles they create.
